Notes

Welcome to my notes! Below you’ll find lecture notes from my master’s at EPFL alongside independent learning notes on topics in theoretical physics and mathematics. Writing things down independently is, I think, the best way to actually understand them — these may contain mistakes and rough edges, but I hope they’re still useful.

Source files live on my GitHub. The note templates are open-sourced as well: LaTeX template · Typst template.

EPFL Lecture Notes

Course notes from my master's at EPFL.

  • QFT ZH Quantum Field Theory 1 PDF · Source
  • QFT EN Quantum Field Theory 2 PDF · Source
  • GR ZH General Relativity and Cosmology 1 PDF · Source
  • GR EN General Relativity and Cosmology 2 PDF · Source
  • QM ZH Quantum Mechanics 3 PDF · Source
  • QM EN Quantum Mechanics 4 PDF · Source
  • QI ZH Quantum Information Theory PDF · Source

Notes

Notes on topics in theoretical physics and mathematics.

Topic
No notes match your filters.

2+1D Gravity

  • EN 2+1 Dimensional Gravity and Chern–Simons Theory evergreen 2+1d gravity and its relation to Chern–Simons theory; a naive real-polarization quantization in the zero-cosmological-constant case. PDF · Source

2D CFT

  • EN Basic CFT evergreen Concise introduction to 2D CFT following the Yellow Book. A starting point — the bootstrapping construction is more systematic. PDF · Source
  • EN Basic BCFT evergreen Boundary CFT by analogy with ordinary QFT. Useful for building intuition before turning to the bootstrap formulation. PDF · Source
  • EN Boundary States of Majorana CFT evergreen Boundary states of Majorana CFT — the simplest example of fermionic CFT with boundary. PDF · Source
  • EN Cardy Lewellen Sewing Constraints in BCFT evergreen Cardy/Lewellen's sewing constraints in 2D CFT with boundary. PDF · Source

QFT

  • EN Core Concepts in QFT growing Core concepts in QFT, including renormalization, anomalies, instantons and solitons. PDF · Source

Symmetry

  • EN Gauging of a Discrete Symmetry growing What is a discrete gauge theory and how to use it to gauge a discrete symmetry? PDF · Source
